name: message_threads
title: Message Threads
description: One conversation: a subject, a denormalized participant snapshot, and the flags an inbox needs (star/archive/trash, an unread rollup, a running message count) -- a faithful port of a private predecessor-system audit's unified-inbox thread model, not part of this repo. That source handled BOTH internal user-to-user DMs and real external email through the same thread shape (thread_type); this package carries that same split even though the external half has no transport wired up yet (see dbbasic-package.json's deferred list). participant_summary is a plain display snapshot, not a participants relation/M2M -- this codebase has no user-directory join built for that, and a snapshot is the same trade-off app-invoices' invoices.customer_name makes for its customer relation. labels is free text (comma-separated), not a labels/taxonomy collection -- same call app-worker's profiles.skills makes for free-text skills, not a relation.

fields.name: thread_type
fields.label: Thread Type
fields.help: internal = a DM between this codebase's own users; email = external mail. Carried from the source model's own split. No IMAP/SMTP sync exists yet (deferred -- see dbbasic-package.json), so an email-type thread's messages are only ever created here by hand, by a seed, or by a future sync job.

fields.name: participant_summary
fields.label: Participants
fields.help: Denormalized display string (e.g. names or addresses, comma-separated) -- not a relation. See this schema's own description for why.

fields.name: message_count
fields.label: Message Count
fields.help: Running total, carried like forum_topics.views: this package does not stamp or increment it -- see dbbasic-package.json's deferred list.

fields.name: is_read
fields.label: Read
fields.help: Unread rollup for the thread as a whole. Defaults true because this package ships no inbound-transport insertion path (IMAP sync is deferred) -- a record created here by hand, seed, or draft save is never something the owner needs to "catch up on". A future sync job is what should set this false on a newly-synced inbound thread. Toggled by the thread page's mark-read action, and independently by each message's own is_read (see schemas/messages.json).

fields.name: is_trashed
fields.label: Trashed
fields.help: v1 posture: a flag the owner sets and unsets, same as is_archived. There is no separate trash-empty/purge behavior built here -- see dbbasic-package.json's deferred list.

fields.name: labels
fields.label: Labels
fields.help: Free text, comma-separated. No labels/taxonomy collection exists -- see this schema's own description.
